WITH high_value_customers AS (SELECT id FROM customers WHERE id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders))) SELECT json_agg(json_build_object('id', id, 'email', email)) FROM customers c JOIN high_value_customers hvc ON c.id = hvc.id;

WITH recursive nested_cte AS ( SELECT id, parent_id, 0 AS level FROM categories UNION ALL SELECT c.id, c.parent_id, level + 1 FROM categories c JOIN nested_cte nc ON c.parent_id = nc.id ) SELECT * FROM nested_cte ORDER BY level;

SELECT id, name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities WHERE activities.user_id = users.id AND date BETWEEN TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND SYSDATE) FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM activities WHERE date > TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd'))
WITH activity_counts AS (SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id) SELECT user_id FROM activity_counts ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 5 OFFSET 3
SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM activities WHERE date BETWEEN TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND SYSDATE ORDER BY user_id ROWNUM <= 5
WITH user_history AS (SELECT user_id, MAX(date) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id) SELECT user_id FROM user_history WHERE MAX(date) IS NULL OR MAX(date) < TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd')

SELECT user_id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities JOIN activities AS act ON act.user_id = activities.user_id AND act.date > activities.date WHERE activities.user_id = :param) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = (SELECT user_id FROM activities WHERE date = TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND user_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM activities))
SELECT user_id, user_name, MAX(date) FILTER (WHERE user_id = :param) AS last_date FROM activities GROUP BY user_id, user_name

WITH ranks AS (SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS rank FROM activities GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) SELECT user_id FROM ranks WHERE rank = (SELECT min_rank FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS min_rank FROM activities WHERE user_id = :param) q) - 1

SELECT MAX(rownum) FROM (SELECT user_id, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities WHERE activities.user_id = users.id)) AS rownum FROM users JOIN activities USING (id)) WHERE user_id = :param
SELECT user_id FROM activities WHERE date > TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= ALL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id)

WITH user_counts AS (SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id) SELECT user_id FROM user_counts ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC OFFSET 3 LIMIT 4
SELECT user_id FROM activities WHERE user_id = :param GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= ALL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id) OR user_id = :param

SELECT * FROM activities WHERE user_id IN (WITH user_counts AS (SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id) SELECT user_id FROM user_counts ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 5) AND date BETWEEN TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND SYSDATE

SELECT user_id FROM activities JOIN activities AS act ON act.user_id = activities.user_id AND act.date < activities.date WHERE date BETWEEN TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND SYSDATE GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= ALL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activities GROUP BY user_id)

SELECT user_id FROM activities JOIN activities AS act ON act.user_id = activities.user_id AND (act.id, act.date) < (activities.id, activities.date) WHERE date BETWEEN TO_DATE(:param, 'yyyy-mm-dd') AND SYSDATE ORDER BY user_id

SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department IN (SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE job_title = :param))

WITH employee_skills AS (SELECT employee_id, skill_name, COUNT(*) AS skill_count FROM employee_skills GROUP BY employee_id, skill_name) SELECT * FROM employees WHERE employee_id IN (SELECT employee_id FROM employee_skills WHERE skill_count >= (SELECT AVG(skill_count) FROM employee_skills))
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total < (SELECT MAX(total) FROM orders WHERE customer_id = :param) - (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders WHERE customer_id = :param)
SELECT * FROM customers WHERE country IN (SELECT country FROM customers ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)

SELECT * FROM customers WHERE city = (SELECT city FROM customers ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department IN (SELECT department FROM employees ORDER BY AVG(salary) DESC LIMIT 1)

WITH sales_performance AS (SELECT product_id, SUM(total) AS sales FROM orders GROUP BY product_id) SELECT * FROM employees WHERE employee_id IN (SELECT employee_id FROM orders WHERE order_id IN (SELECT order_id FROM sales_performance WHERE sales >= (SELECT AVG(sales) FROM sales_performance)))

SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary = (SELECT salary FROM employees WHERE department = :param) AND job_title <> :param

WITH average_orders AS (SELECT customer_id, AVG(total) AS avg_order FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT * FROM customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM average_orders WHERE avg_order >= (SELECT AVG(avg_order) FROM average_orders))
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE job_title = :param OR job_title = (SELECT job_title FROM employees WHERE department = :param)

WITH order_frequency AS (SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS order_freq FROM orders GROUP BY order_id) SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_id IN (SELECT order_id FROM order_frequency WHERE order_freq >= (SELECT AVG(order_freq) FROM order_frequency))
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department IN (SELECT department FROM employees ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AND job_title = :param
